description: Azure resource type to Draw.io stencil mappings. ALWAYS look up a resource type here before constructing any icon path. For resource types NOT listed here, derive the path from the azure2 convention: img/lib/azure2/<category>/<PascalCase_Resource_Name>.svg — but ONLY after confirming the file exists. Never guess the category from the ARM namespace: Microsoft.Web resources live in app_services/, not web/ or compute/. This file lists all known exceptions (wrong category, singular/plural surprises, non-existent files, fallback icons) and container styles.
criticalWarnings: Microsoft.Web/* resources (App Services, App Service Plans, Function Apps) live in app_services/ — NOT in compute/ or web/
App_Service_Plans.svg is in app_services/, NOT compute/. compute/ does not contain this file.
Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults lives in security/Key_Vaults.svg — there is no keyvault/ category in azure2.
Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters (AKS) uses containers/Kubernetes_Services.svg — filename is Kubernetes_Services, NOT Managed_Clusters.
Microsoft.SignalRService/signalR uses web/SignalR.svg (SINGULAR) — NOT other/, NOT integration/, no plural form exists.
Microsoft.ServiceBus/namespaces uses integration/Service_Bus.svg — filename is SINGULAR (Service_Bus, not Service_Buses).
Microsoft.Cache/redis uses databases/Cache_Redis.svg — NOT Redis_Cache.svg, Redis_Caches.svg, or Azure_Cache_for_Redis.svg.
Microsoft.DocumentDB/databaseAccounts uses databases/Azure_Cosmos_DB.svg — icon is named after the product, not the ARM namespace.
Microsoft.ApiManagement/service uses integration/API_Management_Services.svg — ARM type is singular 'service' but the filename is PLURAL.
There is no generic Event_Grid.svg. Use System_Topic.svg, Event_Grid_Topics.svg, or Event_Grid_Domains.svg depending on the sub-type.

Microsoft.AppConfiguration/configurationStores uses img/lib/mscae/App_Configuration.svg — this icon does NOT exist anywhere in the azure2 library. Using any azure2 path (e.g. management_governance/Management_Groups.svg or management_governance/App_Configurations.svg) produces an empty/broken icon.

Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts (Azure AI / Cognitive Services) uses img/lib/azure2/ai_machine_learning/Cognitive_Services.svg — the category is ai_machine_learning (NOT ai_ml, NOT ai/, NOT cognitive_services/). Using ai_ml/ or any other category produces a broken icon.
Always verify a path exists before using it. When in doubt, check https://github.com/jgraph/drawio/tree/dev/src/main/webapp/img/lib/azure2
iconNamingNotes: Most icons use plural names (e.g., Virtual_Networks.svg, Load_Balancers.svg). The exceptions below use SINGULAR names — do not pluralize them. When unsure, check the draw.io azure2 stencil source at https://github.com/jgraph/drawio/tree/dev/src/main/webapp/img/lib/azure2
iconSizingNotes: defaultWidth and defaultHeight define the mxCell bounding box. Do NOT add imageAspect=0 — it stretches non-square SVGs and distorts icons. For icons marked requiresImageAspect1=true (e.g. Application Insights, Subnet), you MUST add imageAspect=1 to the style string to preserve the SVG's native non-square proportions. For all other icons, omit imageAspect entirely. Always use the exact defaultWidth and defaultHeight from this mapping; never calculate or approximate icon dimensions yourself. Most azure2 icons are square; non-square entries list an explicit defaultHeight.

resourceTypes._comment_singularExceptions: These icons use SINGULAR names, breaking the usual plural pattern

Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/subnet.label: Subnet
Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/subnet.note: Subnet.svg is natively wide (~16.998 x 10.175, ratio ~1.67:1) — NOT square. You MUST include imageAspect=1 in the style string when rendering the icon, otherwise it stretches vertically into a distorted square. When used as an isContainer, the icon is typically omitted in favor of the containerStyle, so aspect does not apply.

Microsoft.ServiceBus/namespaces.label: Service Bus
Microsoft.ServiceBus/namespaces.note: SINGULAR filename — Service_Bus.svg, NOT Service_Buses.svg. CATEGORY: integration.

Microsoft.Cache/redis.label: Redis Cache
Microsoft.Cache/redis.note: UNUSUAL NAMING — filename is Cache_Redis.svg (NOT Redis_Cache.svg, Redis_Caches.svg, or Azure_Cache_for_Redis.svg). CATEGORY: databases.

Microsoft.DocumentDB/databaseAccounts.label: Cosmos DB
Microsoft.DocumentDB/databaseAccounts.note: Filename does NOT follow the ARM namespace (no 'DocumentDB' or 'DatabaseAccounts' in it). Uses the friendly product name 'Azure_Cosmos_DB.svg' (SINGULAR).

Microsoft.ApiManagement/service.label: API Management
Microsoft.ApiManagement/service.note: ARM type ends in singular 'service' but the icon filename is PLURAL — API_Management_Services.svg. CATEGORY: integration (NOT apimanagement/ or api/).

resourceTypes._comment_wrongCategory: These icons are in unexpected categories — the LLM would guess wrong

Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults.label: Key Vault
Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults.note: CATEGORY: security — NOT keyvault/, NOT identity/. The Microsoft.KeyVault ARM namespace is misleading; no 'keyvault' category exists in azure2.

Microsoft.ContainerService/managedCluste.label: AKS
Microsoft.ContainerService/managedCluste.note: CATEGORY: containers — NOT containerservice/. Filename is Kubernetes_Services.svg (product name), not Managed_Clusters.svg (ARM name). An identical copy also exists in compute/ — either path renders; prefer containers/.

Microsoft.SignalRService/signalR.label: SignalR
Microsoft.SignalRService/signalR.note: CATEGORY: web — NOT signalrservice/, NOT other/, NOT integration/. Filename is SINGULAR SignalR.svg (no plural form exists).

Microsoft.Web/serverfarms.label: App Service Plan
Microsoft.Web/serverfarms.note: CATEGORY: app_services — distinct icon from App_Services.svg. Do NOT use compute/ (file does not exist there).

Microsoft.Web/sites.label: App Service
Microsoft.Web/sites.note: CATEGORY: app_services — NOT compute, NOT web. The Microsoft.Web ARM namespace is misleading on both counts. For Function Apps (kind contains 'functionapp'), use the Function_Apps.svg entry below instead.

Microsoft.Web/sites[functionapp].label: Function App
Microsoft.Web/sites[functionapp].note: Use this entry when Microsoft.Web/sites has kind containing 'functionapp' (e.g. 'functionapp,linux', 'functionapp'). CATEGORY: compute — Function_Apps.svg lives in compute/, unlike App_Services.svg which is in app_services/. Always check the resource 'kind' field to distinguish Function Apps from regular App Services.

Microsoft.EventGrid/systemTopics.label: Event Grid System Topic
Microsoft.EventGrid/systemTopics.note: Use System_Topic.svg for systemTopics. There is no generic Event_Grid.svg — use Event_Grid_Topics.svg for custom topics, Event_Grid_Domains.svg for domains.

Microsoft.Communication/emailServices.label: Email Communication Services
Microsoft.Communication/emailServices.note: No distinct email icon exists in azure2 — shares ACS icon. Differentiate from communicationServices by label text (e.g., italicize or append ' (Email)' to the mxCell value).

Microsoft.Insights/components.label: Application Insights
Microsoft.Insights/components.note: The Application_Insights.svg has a non-square native ratio. You MUST include imageAspect=1 in the style string. Omitting it or using imageAspect=0 will stretch/distort the icon into a malformed square shape.

Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones.label: Private DNS Zone
Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones.note: No dedicated Private DNS icon exists — uses DNS_Zones.svg as fallback

Microsoft.AppConfiguration/configuration.label: App Configuration
Microsoft.AppConfiguration/configuration.note: LIBRARY: mscae — NOT azure2. This icon does not exist anywhere in the azure2 library. Using any azure2 path produces a broken/empty icon. The correct path is img/lib/mscae/App_Configuration.svg (singular, not plural).

resourceTypes._comment_commonResources: Common resources that follow the usual <category>/<PascalCase_Name>.svg plural convention — listed here so agents never have to guess.

Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworkGateways.label: VPN Gateway
Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworkGateways.note: Same ARM type covers both VPN and ExpressRoute gateways; differentiate via the resource's gatewayType property when labeling (e.g., 'ExpressRoute Gateway').

Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts.label: Cognitive Services
Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts.note: CATEGORY: ai_machine_learning — NOT ai_ml/, NOT ai/, NOT cognitive_services/. Also used as a fallback for Azure AI Foundry and Azure OpenAI when no dedicated stencil exists.

resourceTypes._comment_containers: Container elements with special rendering styles
